private.name: planqc

data.narrative:
QuiX Quantum is a Series A quantum computing company that has raised €15M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 peers across quantum computing—3 public companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Systems) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient granular comparables. While 7 same-sector peers exist in quantum computing, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier peers yielded fewer than three datapoints in each stratum, making a narrow peer multiple unreliable. A cross-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the structural differences between quantum hardware platforms and their respective revenue models and technology maturity levels.

Without triangulation across multiple valuation methods, a single defensible range cannot be supported by the data provided. The analysis would require either: (1) expansion of the comparable set to adjacent quantum-adjacent hardware or deep-tech sectors; (2) bottom-up approaches anchored to R&D spend, IP position, or go-to-market roadmap; or (3) venture-method or scenario modeling tied to specific technical milestones and exit assumptions. The scarcity of same-stage quantum computing peers at Series A reflects the sector's early consolidation and limited financing activity. Further data collection is necessary before issuing a valuation recommendation.
